basics.name: Tiancan Xia
basics.summary: M.Eng. student at Southeast University (SEU), Nanjing, China. Research focuses on Score-based Generative Models for Inverse Problems, Bayesian Inference, and Variational Methods. Expected graduation: June 2027.

work.company: Lab of Efficient Architectures for Digital-communication and Signal-processing (LEADS)
work.position: Research Assistant: Bridging Diffusion Priors and Message Passing
work.summary: Supervisors: Prof. Xiaosi Tan, Prof. Chuan Zhang. Research on connecting diffusion priors and message passing algorithms for inverse problems.
highlights: Proposed a message passing-based diffusion posterior sampling framework for compressive image recovery
Formulated posterior score estimation as a variational inference problem using generalized Tweedie formula
Integrated diffusion timestep states as auxiliary input vectors in message passing iterations to represent evolving generative priors
Designed a unified framework compatible with multiple message passing variants and diffusion samplers
Derived closed-form posterior mean and variance updates for the message passing input step
Achieved competitive reconstruction quality compared to existing zero-shot diffusion-based methods on FFHQ256 dataset

work.company: Lab of Efficient Architectures for Digital-communication and Signal-processing (LEADS)
work.position: Research Assistant: Variational Inference for High-Dimensional Signal Estimation
work.summary: Supervisors: Prof. Xiaosi Tan, Prof. Chuan Zhang. Development of variational Bayesian message passing receivers for uplink ISAC systems and EP-based distributed detectors.
highlights: Developed a message passing framework based on mean-field approximation for joint parameter estimation and signal detection
Extended the framework to Bethe variational free energy and derived noise variance updating equations via stationary point analysis
Analyzed convergence performance and estimation accuracy through state evolution equations
Outperformed existing linear and interference cancellation-based baselines with comparable complexity
Developed a distributed approximate message passing algorithm distributing computational tasks between access points and CPU
Reformulated message passing rules to reduce computational demands while preserving detection accuracy
Achieved 85%-94% complexity reduction compared to existing non-linear EP-based distributed detectors

work.company: Lab of Efficient Architectures for Digital-communication and Signal-processing (LEADS)
work.position: Research Assistant: Factor Graph-based Joint Optimization of Baseband Detection and Decoding
work.summary: Supervisors: Prof. Xiaosi Tan, Prof. Chuan Zhang. Research on efficient iterative detection and decoding receivers for polar-coded MIMO systems.
highlights: Employed expectation propagation with approximation to eliminate matrix inversions during detection
Introduced a modified belief propagation flip decoder based on critical sets to resolve error floors
Optimized message passing mechanisms between detector and decoder to improve BER performance
Introduced a novel architecture based on joint factor graphs enabling mini-batch information exchange
Restructured the EP with successive updating algorithm in a layered manner
Designed a sorting and grouping strategy to optimize the symbol update process
Demonstrated superior performance across various channel conditions compared to existing methods

interests.name: Research Interests
keywords: Score-based Generative Models for Inverse Problems

publications.name: Variational Bayesian Message Passing Receiver for Uplink ISAC Systems
publications.summary: First author. Proposed variational Bayesian message passing receivers for uplink integrated sensing and communication systems.

publications.name: Jointly-Layered Iterative Detection and Decoding for Massive MIMO: An EP-SU Based Framework
publications.summary: First author. Proposed a jointly-layered iterative detection and decoding algorithm for coded MIMO systems based on joint factor graphs.

publications.name: EP-based Distributed Message Passing Detection for Cell-Free Massive MIMO
publications.summary: First author. Proposed expectation propagation-based distributed detectors for cell-free massive MIMO systems with 85%-94% complexity reduction.

publications.name: An Efficient Iterative Detection and Decoding Receiver for Polar-Coded Massive MIMO
publications.publisher: 57th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
publications.summary: First author. Proposed an efficient iterative detection and decoding receiver using EP approximation and BP flip decoder for polar-coded MIMO.

publications.name: AttnMPNet: An attention-based message passing network for MIMO detection
publications.summary: Co-author. Developed an attention-based message passing network for MIMO detection.

publications.name: Turbo product code-aided spatiotemporal 2-D coded MIMO for URLLC
publications.summary: Co-author. Proposed turbo product code-aided spatiotemporal 2-D coded MIMO for ultra-reliable low-latency communications.

publications.name: Low-complexity double EP approximation for parallel detection-decoding in MIMO systems
publications.summary: Co-author. Proposed low-complexity double EP approximation for parallel detection-decoding in MIMO systems.

publications.name: A soft iterative receiver with simplified EP detection for coded MIMO systems
publications.publisher: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
publications.summary: Co-author. Proposed a soft iterative receiver with simplified EP detection for coded MIMO systems.
